data.body:
G3 child-coverage audit (parent f89d-e54d-203d-4ee0 Ă— THIS child 8937 "Producerâ†”consumer snapshot contract: shared schema + round-trip test through production path"), 4-bucket per parent criterion, cross-checked against the WHOLE sibling roster (2359/33d0/59ef/822a/8937/fe57). VERDICT: clean pairing â€” no G3 coverage finding attributable to this child; no uncovered parent criterion remains across the roster.

OWNED CRITERIA â€” FULLY covered by THIS child (8937 is the keystone + owns the guardrail artifact):
- Parent AC "A single-source-of-truth snapshot-entry schema module exists as the canonical shape" â†’ 8937 AC1 (`_snapshot_schema.py` defines the snapshot-entry shape, TypedDict + JSON Schema, as the SSOT). FULLY.
- Parent AC "A test validates the fetcher's snapshot output against that schema" â†’ 8937 AC2 (test validates `fetcher.fetch_snapshot` output against the schema, and FAILS on the pre-fix conditions: issuelinks absent; comment under the wrong key). FULLY.
- Parent AC "A test validates each differ's snapshot reads against that schema" â†’ COVERED by 8937 AC1+AC3: the schema is declared "covering every field the differs read" and the round-trip drives BOTH the inbound_differ and outbound_differ (repo: inbound_differ.py + outbound_differ.py) reading from the schema-validated snapshot through the production path, asserting each consumer-depended key/shape (comment, issuelinks, parent + scalars) is read correctly. The differs' reads are validated against the canonical shape both sides target. FULLY (covered-by-construction via the schema SSOT + joint round-trip).
- Parent AC "One round-trip contract test exercises fetcher â†’ snapshot â†’ differs â†’ applier through the production path, using fixtures derived from real Jira payloads" â†’ 8937 AC3 (round-trip test runs fetcher â†’ differs â†’ applier through the production path, asserting comment/issuelinks/parent + scalars; fails if a producer key/shape diverges from a consumer read) + fe57 (real-payload fixtures + FakeAcliClient foundation; 8937 depends_on fe57). FULLY.
- Parent AC "A documented anti-change-detector guardrail for reconciler tests is committed and referenced by new tests (owned by story B)" â†’ 8937 IS story B and OWNS this: 8937 description "Owns the epic's guardrail artifact (resolves the roster-level gap): commit a documented anti-change-detector guardrail â€¦ a short docs/ note (ADR-style) â€¦ New tests reference it," and 8937 AC5 "A documented anti-change-detector guardrail (ADR-style note recording the testing-strategy decision + the schema location + the rules) is committed and referenced by the new tests." FULLY (the parent AC names its owner verbatim).
- Parent SC "Producer (fetcher + enrichment) and consumers (differs/appliers) cannot drift on snapshot shape without a test failing" â†’ 8937 OWNS the keystone half (schema SSOT + producerâ†’consumer round-trip), complemented by fe57 (honest fixtures/fake) and 59ef (verified-fake shape fidelity). FULLY.
- CONTRIBUTED: parent SC "New tests assert behavior/contract (semantic round-trip, structure/type); the suite stays lean; zero change-detector tests added" via 8937 AC4 (all assertions semantic â€” keys/types/round-trip â€” never whole-blob golden equality or interaction/mock-call-count) AND via the AC5 guardrail that GOVERNS new reconciler tests; parent AC "all six child stories closed; full reconciler suite green" via this child landing green.

DELEGATION â€” parent criteria this child does NOT own are correctly assigned to named siblings; this child's declared OUT items (capturing fixtures â†’ story A/fe57; collapsing appliers â†’ story D/33d0; telemetry â†’ story E/2359) are consistent with the roster:
- AC "Outbound application is collapsed to a single production path (no parallel applier remains)" + AC "The retired outbound applier path is deleted" + SC "Outbound sync is exercised through the production code path, not a parallel dead path" â†’ 33d0 (AC1/AC2).
- AC "Outbound sub-op telemetry (links_applied/comments_applied/labels_applied) surfaced in the structured batch outcome" + AC "computed > 0 && applied == 0 fails loudly, after the warn-first rollout" + SC "A silent sub-op no-op fails a test/canary instead of passing green" â†’ 2359 (AC1/AC2/AC3).
- AC "Bidirectional comment/link/parent round-trip asserted hermetically through the production path" + AC "Every probe assertion that encodes a current bug as expected is converted to fail-when-the-gap-closes" + SC "Bidirectional round-trips â€¦ asserted; no probe encodes a bug as expected" â†’ 822a (AC1/AC2).

ANTI-FP / WITHDRAWAL of the recurring AC6 false positive: earlier audits across the roster (and on siblings) raised "Parent AC6 (documented anti-change-detector guardrail) only PARTIALLY covered â€” no sibling produces a governing artifact." That finding is a FALSE POSITIVE and stays WITHDRAWN. It was raised against a paraphrased "governs new tests" wording that does not match the plan text; the actual parent AC reads verbatim "A documented anti-change-detector guardrail for reconciler tests is committed and referenced by new tests (owned by story B)" â€” it (a) explicitly NAMES its owner (story B = THIS child 8937) and (b) is FULLY covered here by 8937's description + AC5. Under the G3 ANTI-FP rule (a criterion covered-by-definition by a NAMED consumer counts), this is FULLY covered, not partial. Sibling audits on 2359/33d0/59ef/822a have already withdrawn the same false positive.

4-bucket summary for this pairing:
- FULLY (OWNED by THIS child): parent AC "SSOT snapshot-entry schema module"; parent AC "fetcher snapshot validated vs schema"; parent AC "each differ's reads validated vs schema" (via schema SSOT + joint round-trip); parent AC "round-trip contract through production path w/ real fixtures"; parent AC "documented anti-change-detector guardrail committed + referenced (owned by story B)"; parent SC "producer/consumers cannot drift on snapshot shape."
- CONTRIBUTED: parent SC "zero change-detector / lean suite" (8937 AC4 + AC5 guardrail); parent AC "suite green."
- COVERED-BY-NAMED-SIBLING: applier-collapse + retired-path-deletion + production-path-exercise (33d0); sub-op telemetry + silent-no-op canary (2359); hermetic bidirectional round-trip + probe de-encoding (822a); real-payload fixtures/fake foundation (fe57); verified-fake shape fidelity (59ef).
- STRUCTURAL/AGGREGATE: parent AC "all six child stories closed; full reconciler suite green."
- UNCOVERED: none.

No G3 coverage finding for this pairing. The union of children covers every parent acceptance/success criterion, and THIS child fully delivers the largest owned share (schema SSOT, fetcher+differ validation, the production-path round-trip contract, and the documented anti-change-detector guardrail referenced by new tests).
